title: isoloma inaequale
intro: Isoloma inaequale is a plant species, also known as the "Samoan bitterwood," which belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Isoloma inaequale include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and even exhibit antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used in teas, infusions, or as a raw ingredient in traditional Samoan medicine, often in combination with other medicinal plants.
